config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menindas orang per orang. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat gotong royong itu penting, tetapi gotong royong yang sehat justru menghormati martabat dan kebebasan tiap individu. Kepentingan umum memang perlu diperhatikan, tetapi tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar seseorang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be treated as automatically secondary to society’s interests. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that gives “society” too much power, because history shows that governments and majorities can use that language to trample dissent, minority rights, and basic freedoms. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public safety and shared rules do matter, but the burden should always be on society to justify any limit. If the priority is reversed, it becomes too easy to excuse oppression in the name of the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia saya melihat “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau berbeda pendapat. Gotong royong memang nilai penting, tetapi gotong royong yang sehat justru berjalan bersama perlindungan hak individu, bukan mengorbankannya begitu saja. Kepentingan umum boleh membatasi hak dalam kondisi tertentu, misalnya demi keselamatan publik, tetapi pembatasan itu harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, istilah “demi masyarakat” mudah berubah menjadi alat pembenaran untuk sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that framing is dangerous because “the common good” has too often been used to silence minorities, punish dissent, and excuse abuse by governments or majorities. The better standard is that individual rights should be the default priority, and any limits on them must be narrow, necessary, and openly justified. Society matters, but it is strongest when it protects people rather than treating them as expendable for a vague collective goal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, masalahnya bukan pada gotong royongnya, tetapi pada kecenderungan memakai kata "demi masyarakat" untuk menutup-nutupi ketidakadilan terhadap orang yang lemah, berbeda, atau tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan umum memang bisa membatasi hak, tetapi hanya jika jelas alasannya, benar-benar perlu, dan tidak berlebihan; kalau tidak, itu gampang berubah jadi alat sewenang-wenang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan martabat tiap orang, bukan dari pengorbanan hak individu tanpa batas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, that framing is too easy to abuse, because our history shows how “the common good” can become a cover for suppressing dissent, minority groups, and unpopular speech. Society does matter, but its legitimacy depends on protecting people’s rights first and only limiting them in narrow, necessary, and accountable ways. If the collective always comes first, the individual becomes too vulnerable to whatever the majority or government decides is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
